ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Filing Of Michele Silva'S Accounting Petition.
Off Calendar Jun-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young for and with Mark Mussen.

Ms. Young stated that there were issues with the Windell accounting regarding the Sonoma property as to rental income and a remodel. In addition, she stated that she had requested payment of $9,994.00 from Ms. Silva, that Ms. Silva stated she would look into the rent, and that she doesn't owe anything on the remodel. Ms. Young stated that she and her client disagree with the statement that Ms. Silva doesn't owe anything on the remodel. The Court stated that the amount in dispute was approximately $10,000.00. Ms. Young stated that they probably wouldn't get anymore information and confirmed for the Court that they were asking for the $9,994.00 to be paid by Ms. Silva. In addition, she stated that she would be happy to file a formal objection. The matter was taken under submission. Ms. Young is to prepare an order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy S. McDaniels.

Off Calendar Nov-24-2008 Continued To Dec-15-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Of Beneficiary To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Successor Trustee, And For Payment Of Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Nov-26-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young for Mark Mussen.

Ms. Young informed the Court that she has received nothing in writing as to the voluntary resignation of Michele Silva as Trustee. The Court indicated there were two options to either give her more time or grant the petition. Ms. Young requested the Court go forward. The Court inquired as to the accounting by Ms. Silva. Ms Young stated that Mr. Windell had informed her that the accounting was mailed out on Friday. The Court issued an Order to Show Cause to Michele Silva for failure to file the accounting with a return date of December 15, 2008 and granted the petition to remove Michele Silva as Trustee (subject to a written order). In addition, the Court stated that the trusts were not going to be under Court supervision, which Ms. Young confirmed. Ms. Young stated that she wanted to keep Ms. Silva's accounting under Court supervision, but noted there was no need for supervision as to Wells Fargo. Ms. Young to submit the proposed order.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Linda Forster, CSR#13286. Clerk: Jackie Alameda/ S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Filing Of Michele Silva'S Accounting Petition.
Off Calendar Oct-14-2008 Continued To Nov-24-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Of Beneficiary To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Successor Trustee, And For Payment Of Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Oct-14-2008 Continued To Nov-24-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young for and with Mark Mussen (Beneficiary).

The Court stated that the order submitted for the last hearing had no underlying petition for relief sought and that Ms. Young needed to file an amendment to the petition with proper notice. Ms. Young stated that Wells Fargo had consented to act as Successor Trustee and that she had not heard from Ms. Silva regarding her voluntary resignation. The hearing on Michele's removal and appointment of Wells Fargo Bank was continued to November 24, 2008 for status on filing of the amendment to the petition. The status hearing set for November 3, 2008 on the filing of Ms. Silva's accounting petition was also continued to November 24, 2008.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Mann.

Petition Of Beneficiary To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Successor Trustee, And For Payment Of Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Sep-22-2008 Continued To Oct-14-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Young with and for Mark Mussen.

Michele Silva (Respondent) was not present in court. Ms. Young informed the Court that Ms. Silva was now being represented by Carl Wendell and that they had agreed that Ms. Silva would step down and that Wells Fargo would serve as Successor Trustee. In addition, Ms. Young stated that Ms. Silva and Mr. Wendell agreed to pay her attorney's fees. The Trustee's powers are suspended pending the appointment of Wells Fargo as Successor Trustee. The status on filing of Wells Fargo's consent is set for October 14, 2008. The request for payment of attorney's fees was granted (subject to written order). The Court ordered Ms. Silva to file her accounting by November 3, 2008. Upon filing of Wells Fargo's consent, the petition to remove the Trustee will be taken off calendar.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Padilla, CSR 975. Clerk: R. Noguera/ J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy T. Aguerre.
